visual_base.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video opens in a brightly lit Costco warehouse. A young woman with long dark hair, wearing a brown utility-style jacket and a plaid skirt, stands in the aisle holding a large green dinosaur toy box. Behind her, shelves are stocked with products like Play-Doh, and several other shoppers are visible in the background moving through the aisles. A large 'Costco Wholesale' logo is superimposed at the top of the frame. (2) The camera zooms in for a medium close-up as the woman points to the 'Big Dinosaur Family' toy. The toy features a large green T-Rex with a transparent belly containing 28 smaller dinosaur figurines. The woman touches the dinosaur's snout and teeth, demonstrating its physical appearance. The industrial warehouse lighting remains consistent, highlighting the colorful packaging. (3) The perspective shifts to the back and side of the box. The woman points at a 'Product List' chart showing different dinosaur species and instructional graphics that detail how to assemble the tail and activate sound features. She mimics the action of pressing the dinosaur's arm to open its belly. The scene concludes with her holding the box toward the camera, smiling warmly.
visual_base.text_narrative_log: (1) [Logo]: COSTCO WHOLESALE. [Variety]: dinosaur. (2) [Subtitle]: 嗚嗚嗚 dinosaur. [Logo]: COSTCO WHOLESALE. [Variety]: dinosaur. (3) [Subtitle]: 最新出的 恐龍家族 裡面有28隻小恐龍模型 759元 妹妹最喜歡恐龍了 哇你有看到嗎 滿滿的恐龍 有沒有 聖誕節交換禮物要到了. (4) [Logo]: J'adore. [Slogan]: STEAM PLUS TOYS. [Subtitle]: 嗯剛好 你班上28個同學 你一人送一隻 然後這個最大的模型 你剛好留著 還是有戴小墨鏡的哦 帥哦 然後旁邊還有這個耳罩機啊 裡面有28隻小恐龍 然後配上這最外面的 所以總共29隻 這樣759元 你覺得值得嗎. (5) [Logo]: J'adore. [Subtitle]: 這就是它的所有小恐龍 很可愛耶 既可以收藏 然後又有滿滿的恐龍啊 哦它的手是 你這樣按它的手 它肚子就會打開 哇 你就可以拿開. (6) [Subtitle]: 不過它現場 他會把它封起來 就是你買回家的時候 你可以這樣一壓 然後它就蹦 然后恐龍就蹦. (7) [Slogan]: STEAM PLUS TOYS. [Subtitle]: 這個尾巴呢 你要自己裝上去 然後它的嘴巴打開之後 會有聲光效果 吼 然後接下來是小手手 一壓恐龍就會出來玩 超可愛的 很值得.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator, approximately 7 other shoppers visible in the background aisles throughout the video.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Asian woman with long dark hair, wearing a brown padded utility jacket, a white top, and a plaid skirt.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: The interior of a Costco warehouse, featuring high ceilings, industrial metal shelving units filled with boxed toys (Play-Doh), and signs for 'Cakes', 'Pastries', and 'Meat'.

audio_base.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a high-energy pop music bed featuring female vocals singing the word 'Dinosaur' in a melodic hook. The music is characterized by sharp electronic percussion and bright synthesizer textures. (2) A male voice enters with a professional, clear, and friendly tone, speaking in Mandarin at a moderate speed. He provides a rhythmic narration about the toy, maintaining a consistent pitch. The background music ducks slightly to allow the speech to remain the primary focus. (3) A subtle 'ding' sound effect occurs as the narrator mentions the price of the toy. The acoustic environment is clean and sterile, indicating a high-quality post-production mix with no significant ambient noise. (4) The narrator's tone becomes more enthusiastic as he describes the '28 mini dinosaurs' inside. The phrase ends with a slight increase in vocal arousal. The music continues with a steady four-on-the-floor beat, providing a constant rhythmic drive until the end of the clip.

narrative_and_purpose.narrativeStructure: List-based: Hook -> Feature Presentation -> Price/Value Proposition -> CTA
narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video serves as a promotional showcase for a dinosaur-themed toy set found at Costco. It combines visual product demonstration with a spoken value analysis, highlighting its suitability as a Christmas gift for children based on its price and quantity of contents.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: Direct display of the product package, mentions of specific price points (759元), and clear naming of the retailer (Costco).

high_inference.freeDescribe: The video effectively utilizes social media editing trends, including variety-show style text and high-arousal narration, to maintain engagement while presenting a retail product.
